author: @XrayNut
text: I'm going to sue the manufacturer of my truck because they did not tell me that it's not Okay to start the engine, put the transmission in gear, then get out of the truck, step around the front of it and let it run me over. Oh, that's NOT Okay to do? Yes, some people (thankfully not very many) are THAT STUPID. Anyhow, good talk. My only suggestion concerns the sound quality. Either find a better location that doesn't echo so badly, and/or wear a lapel mic that is close to your mouth.  Thanks!

author: @VenkateshksDr
text:
sorry thought this might be appropriate
If you can keep your head when all about you
  Are losing theirs and blaming it on you,
If you can trust yourself when all men doubt you,
  But make allowance for their doubting too;
If you can wait and not be tired by waiting,
  Or being lied about, don’t deal in lies,
Or being hated, don’t give way to hating,
  And yet don’t look too good, nor talk too wise:

If you can dream—and not make dreams your master;
  If you can think—and not make thoughts your aim;
If you can meet with Triumph and Disaster
  And treat those two impostors just the same;
If you can bear to hear the truth you've spoken
  Twisted by knaves to make a trap for fools,
Or watch the things you gave your life to, broken,
  And stoop and build ’em up with worn-out tools:

If you can make one heap of all your winnings
  And risk it on one turn of pitch-and-toss,
And lose, and start again at your beginnings
  And never breathe a word about your loss;
If you can force your heart and nerve and sinew
  To serve your turn long after they are gone,
And so hold on when there is nothing in you
  Except the Will which says to them: “Hold on!”

If you can talk with crowds and keep your virtue,
  Or walk with Kings—nor lose the common touch,
If neither foes nor loving friends can hurt you,
  If all men count with you, but none too much;
If you can fill the unforgiving minute
  With sixty seconds’ worth of distance run,
Yours is the Earth and everything that’s in it,
  And—which is more—you’ll be a Man, my son![6]

author: @onesleepyshadow
text:
Nice rant. Reminds me of the stories my mom would tell me growing up. She was a commercial underwriter (insurance) and would get some crazy claims. Two that come to mind:

Two guys were riding in a truck. One of them had something stuck in his eye. He asked his buddy next to him to see if he could get it out with a screw driver. One bump in the road later and you can guess what happened. 

Another one was a guy working on a road crew. His job was to direct a truck where to dump asphalt. He told them to dump it right where he was standing. He's now part of the road.

  I don't know how you protect yourself from people without commonsense.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - loved the stories - i am not sure you can protect yourself from them lol

author: @johntaylor6061
text:
Hey Robert,

On IndiGoGo you mentioned, QUOTE:"I am always happy to answer any questions you might have". So I want to take an advantage of opportunity :)

I've just read this paper, "Defect-induced plating of lithium metal within porous graphene networks" Rahul Mukherjee et al. http://homepages.rpi.edu/~koratn/resources/publications/ncomm2.pdf

They start with preparing graphene oxide paper from graphene oxide itself (electrodeposition, see details in section called Method in paper). But mine question is about how hard to mass produce graphene oxide they used in industrial quantities. More specifically, if someone would be producing lets say a thousand tons of GO a year, could you please be so kind as to estimate a ballpack of expenses they would be looking at to produce an extra ton of GO? Well, lets leave capital equipment costs and it depreciation out of the scope of the question. And gross margins. I'm looking for rough ballpark estimate.

I understand it is really hard to answer, but your best guess would be really appreciated. You definitely know more then me about chemistry and industrial processes:)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: John - Seriously? you are asking me to estimate the production costs of a GO production facility. The answer is either useless to you because it is a wild guestimate or will take days to answer properly - I am happy to help - but not to be taken advantage of.

replies.author: @johntaylor6061
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith Well, I'll settle for a "wild guestimate". I'm(and I *was*) definitely not looking for a proper answer:)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
John Taylor
Am I being weird here? It strikes me as a bit pointless but ok - i guess anywhere between 2,000 and 250,000 - you really just haven't given me enough info - i mean key would be what production process do you plan to use? in the paper you reference they just bought theirs - there are a whole host of factors to consider to give any answer relevance - I know this is not what you are looking for but you asking me how long is a piece of string that you are thinking about - I can't really help answer that as I have no idea how long the string is and you are just thinking about it

author: @Candyman97
text: I liked how you cracked your self up, about the guy scratching himself with the chainsaw!

author: @PaulBrunt
text:
Nice one!! I will always remember back in school(aged 14). A guy in my class dipped his hand in the "Sulfuric acid" we'd been given. Everyone was shocked he'd done that! For the previous 6 weeks we'd been told this was the most dangerous stuff on the planet, and it'll "melt your brain" :-)

In that same six weeks the guys Dad had explained the reality to him...it was no worse then the vinegar he put on his chips. I think that single lesson on that single day was more influential to the people in that room than anything the "chemistry" teacher said for that entire year.

The saddest thing about health and safety isn't the rules or regulation; it's the stifling of education that really kick's you in the balls :-(

author: @JenniferKandel
text: Whenever I need a fresh perspective on a problem, I'll ask one or all five of our teenagers for an opinion, works every time. Thank you for giving their amazing minds the credit they are due :)
